# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security


import { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next';  // assuming you're using nextjs for serverless functions with NodeJS environment (not vercel)
const { PassThrough } = require('stream');  
// importing pass through stream to create a dummy readable and writable streams. You can replace this part if your use case requires more complex behavior like buffering or compressing data before sending it back 
import * as crypto from 'crypto';    // for generating random string in headers, you may need these dependencies based on the requirement of security features (like hmac)  
const algorithm = 'aes-256-cbc' ;// encryption key length should be same with your server side setup. 
let secretKey;     // declare a variable to hold our encrypted data and decrypted keys, you can use environment variables or some other secure way of handling this in production level code  
const iv = crypto.randomBytes(16);    // initialisation vector for encryption/decryption process should be same as your server side setup 
class WSResponse extends PassThrough {     // extend pass through stream to create a custom writable streams class, you can use this if there is any specific behavior required in response streaming.  
constructor(secret: string) {    super();      secretKey = crypto.createSecretKey(algorithm,{key: Buffer.from(secret)});  }     // constructor to initialize the encryption key and iv for every new WSResponse instance created by this class       write (chunk: any , encoding?: BufferEncoding | undefined): void {      const cipher = crypto.createCipheriv(algorithm, secretKey,iv );    let encryptedData =  cipher.update(chunk);     if (!encryptedData) 
{   return; }       // encrypt the data and write it to stream using WSResponse class instance      this._writableState = {};        const finalBuffer  = Buffer.concat([ iv,cipher.final() ] );    super.write(finalBuffer);     if (this.length === 0) { 
super.end(); }   // end the writestream when all data has been written to it       this._writableState = {};        const finalBuffer  = Buffer.concat([ iv,cipher.final() ] );    super.write(finalBuffer);     if (this.length === 0) { 
super.end(); }   // end the writestream when all data has been written to it       this._writableState = {};        const finalBuffer  = Buffer
